name: portfolio-tracker
title: Portfolio Tracker
description: Track value of your Stocks, ETFs, Properties, Cash, Debt and Crypto with automatic price updates over time to see your net worth
author: filipawaits

commands.name: portfolio
commands.title: Portfolio Tracker
commands.description: Track your investment portfolio and net worth across multiple accounts

commands.name: search-investments
commands.title: Search Investments
commands.subtitle: Portfolio Tracker
commands.description: Search for stocks ETFs, and funds to add to your portfolio

commands.name: fire
commands.title: FIRE Dashboard
commands.subtitle: Portfolio Tracker
commands.description: Financial Independence, Retire Early — track your progress to financial freedom

tools.name: get-portfolio-summary
tools.title: Get Portfolio Summary
tools.description: Returns a complete overview of the user's investment portfolio including all accounts, positions, asset type allocation, currencies held, and position counts. Use this to answer questions about portfolio composition, holdings, and asset allocation. This tool takes no arguments — call it with an empty input {}.

tools.name: get-account-details
tools.title: Get Account Details
tools.description: Returns detailed information about a specific portfolio account identified by name. Includes all positions within that account with their full details. Use when the user asks about a specific account.

tools.name: get-position-details
tools.title: Get Position Details
tools.description: Returns detailed information about a specific position identified by name or Yahoo Finance symbol. Searches across all accounts. Use when the user asks about a specific holding or investment.

tools.name: get-debt-summary
tools.title: Get Debt Summary
tools.description: Returns a focused summary of all debt positions including balances, APR, monthly repayments, and repayment status. Use when the user asks about their debts, total debt, or repayment progress. This tool takes no arguments — call it with an empty input {}.

tools.name: get-fire-projection
tools.title: Get FIRE Projection
tools.description: Returns the user's current FIRE baseline: settings, contributions, projection timeline, Coast FIRE analysis, and lump sum solver table. Use ONLY for read-only questions about the current state — 'When will I reach FIRE?', 'What is my FIRE number?', 'Can I coast now?', 'How much to retire 3 years earlier?'. Do NOT use this tool if the user mentions any change, addition, increase, decrease, or hypothetical ('if I add', 'what if', 'if I contributed', 'if growth drops') — use simulate-fire-scenario instead. This tool takes no arguments — call it with an empty input {}.

tools.name: simulate-fire-scenario
tools.title: Simulate FIRE Scenario
tools.description: Runs a custom FIRE projection with overrides and compares it against the user's current baseline. Use this tool whenever the user mentions ANY change or hypothetical — 'if I add £4000/month', 'what if I increase contributions', 'if growth drops to 5%', 'if I invested a lump sum', 'if I retired at 55', 'if I stop contributing at 45'. This tool self-loads the baseline, so you do NOT need to call get-fire-projection first. Available overrides: monthlyContribution (replace total), additionalMonthlyContribution (add to current), currentPortfolioValue, targetValue, annualGrowthRate, annualInflation, withdrawalRate, yearOfBirth, coastFromAge, targetRetirementAge.

ai.instructions:
Portfolio Tracker is a personal finance extension that tracks stocks, ETFs, properties, cash, and debt across multiple accounts. All data is stored locally — there are no external accounts or servers. Prices refresh daily via Yahoo Finance.

TOOL CALLING RULES:
- Always call ONE tool at a time and wait for its result before deciding whether another tool call is needed.
- Never chain tool calls speculatively. Read the result of the first tool before calling a second.
- This is critical for compatibility with all AI models.

FIRE MATH (all formulas — use these directly, do not re-derive):
  Real growth rate:        r = (nominalGrowthRate - inflationRate) / 100
  FIRE Number:             FN = annualSpending × (100 / withdrawalRate)
  Year-by-year growth:     V(n) = V(n-1) × (1 + r) + C × (1 + r/2)
    where C = annualContribution (monthly × 12), r/2 = half-year contribution approximation
  Time to FIRE (approx):   n ≈ log((FN × r + C) / (P × r + C)) / log(1 + r)
  Coast FIRE balance:      Coast_FN = FN / (1 + r)^yearsToRetirement
  Coast retirement age:    n_coast = log(FN / P) / log(1 + r); retireYear = currentYear + n_coast
  Lump sum solver:         P_required = (FN - C×(1+r/2)×((1+r)^N-1)/r) / (1+r)^N; Lump_Sum = P_required - P_current  (N = targetYear - currentYear)
  Safe withdrawal income:  annualIncome = FN × (withdrawalRate / 100)

COAST FIRE QUESTIONS — two common forms:
  Q1 'If I stop contributing now, when can I retire?'
     → n_coast = log(FN / P) / log(1 + r); retirementYear = currentYear + n_coast
  Q2 'When can I start coasting to retire at age/year T?'
     → requiredCoastBalance = FN / (1 + r)^(T - currentYear)
     → Find first year where V(n) with contributions >= requiredCoastBalance using Formula V(n)

TOOL ROUTING RULE:
- get-fire-projection: ONLY for read-only baseline questions (current state, Coast FIRE, lump sum table). No inputs.
- simulate-fire-scenario: ANY question involving a change or hypothetical (contributions, growth rate, lump sum, coast age, retirement age). It loads the baseline itself — do NOT call get-fire-projection first.
When in doubt, prefer simulate-fire-scenario.

Other rules:
- Portfolio values from tools may not reflect live market prices (cached daily). Note this when discussing exact values.
- Debt balances are subtracted from portfolio value to produce net worth.
- Property positions use UK HPI data for appreciation.
- Holdings in different currencies are converted to the base currency (default GBP) using live FX rates.
- SIPP/pension access age is configurable (UK default: 57).

evals.input: @portfolio-tracker If I stop contributing today, when could I retire?

mocks.get-fire-projection:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
MATHEMATICAL REFERENCE (use these for all calculations)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CORE FORMULAS:

  1. Real Growth Rate:
     r = (nominalGrowthRate - inflationRate) / 100
     r = (7.00% - 2.50%) / 100
     r = 0.045000  (4.50% real return)

  6. Coast FIRE Number (portfolio needed to reach FN with zero contributions):
     Coast_FIRE_Number(n) = FN / (1 + r)^n

  7. Coast FIRE retirement age (if coasting from NOW with current portfolio P):
     n_coast = log(FN / P) / log(1 + r)
     retirementYear = currentYear + n_coast

CURRENT VALUES (substitute into formulas above):
  P  (current portfolio)   = £200,000.00
  FN (FIRE Number / target)= £750,000.00
  r  (real growth rate)    = 0.045000
  C  (annual contribution) = £18,000.00 (£1,500.00/month × 12)
  currentYear              = 2025
  yearOfBirth              = 1990
  withdrawalRate           = 4.00%

FIRE PROGRESS:
  Progress to FIRE: 26.7%  (£200,000.00 of £750,000.00)
  Remaining gap:    £550,000.00

FIRE PROJECTION:
  Current Portfolio Value: £200,000.00
  Target Value:            £750,000.00
  FIRE Year:               2038
  FIRE Age:                48
  Calendar Days to FIRE:   4,745
  Working Days to FIRE:    3,150

COAST FIRE ANALYSIS:
─────────────────────
Q1 — If I stop contributing TODAY, when can I retire?
  Formula: n = log(FN / P) / log(1 + r)
           n = log(£750,000.00 / £200,000.00) / log(1 + 0.045000)
           n = 30.57 years from now
  → Coast retirement year: 2056
  → Coast retirement age:  66
  → This is 18 year(s) LATER than your current FIRE year (2038).
    Coasting now means trading monthly contributions for working a bit longer.

evals.input: @portfolio-tracker When can I start coasting if I want to retire at 55?

mocks.get-fire-projection:
CURRENT VALUES (substitute into formulas above):
  P  (current portfolio)   = £200,000.00
  FN (FIRE Number / target)= £750,000.00
  r  (real growth rate)    = 0.045000
  C  (annual contribution) = £18,000.00 (£1,500.00/month × 12)
  currentYear              = 2025
  yearOfBirth              = 1990

COAST FIRE ANALYSIS:
─────────────────────
Q2 — When can I start coasting to retire at age 48 (2038)?
  Formula: Required_Coast_Balance = FN / (1 + r)^n
           Required_Coast_Balance = £750,000.00 / (1 + 0.045000)^13
           Required_Coast_Balance = £426,508.32

  → You can start coasting in: 2033 (at age 43)
  → That is 8 year(s) from now.
  → You could enjoy 5 year(s) of coast mode before full FIRE.
  Current gap to Coast FIRE Number: £226,508.32
  (46.9% of the way to Coast FIRE)

Q2b — Coast FIRE balance required for common retirement ages:
  Retire at 55 (2045): need £312,448.17 — 64.0% there (gap: £112,448.17)
  Retire at 57 (2047): need £286,047.22 — 69.9% there (gap: £86,047.22)
  Retire at 60 (2050): need £247,831.44 — 80.7% there (gap: £47,831.44)
  Retire at 65 (2055): need £198,272.10 — ✅ already reached!

evals.input: @portfolio-tracker What if I stop contributing at age 45 and coast to retirement?

mocks.simulate-fire-scenario:
FIRE What-If Scenario Comparison
================================

FORMULAS USED:
  Real rate: r = (7 - 2.5) / 100 = 0.045000
  Year-by-year: V(n) = V(n-1) × (1 + r) + C × (1 + r/2)
    where C = annual contribution = £18,000.00/year
  Coast FIRE balance: Coast_Balance = FN / (1 + r)^yearsToRetirement

COAST FIRE SCENARIO:
  Stop contributing at age: 45 (year 2035)
  Portfolio at coast start: £368,124.50
  Formula: n = log(FN / V_coast) / log(1 + r)
           n = log(£750,000.00 / £368,124.50) / log(1 + 0.045000)
           n = 16.41 years from coast start
  → Coast FIRE year: 2051
  → Coast FIRE age:  61

Changes Applied:
  • Coast Mode: stop contributing at age 45

Baseline (Current Settings):
  Monthly Contributions: £1,500.00
  Annual Contributions: £18,000.00
  Real Growth Rate: 4.50%
  FIRE Year: 2038
  FIRE Age: 48
  Days to FIRE: 4,745

Impact:
  FIRE reached 13 year(s) LATER (age 61 vs 48).
  Coasting from age 45 delays retirement by 13 years compared to continuing contributions.

evals.input: @portfolio-tracker How much would I need to invest today to retire 3 years earlier?

mocks.get-fire-projection:
LUMP SUM SOLVER — capital needed today to retire N years earlier:
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Formula: P_required = [ FN - C×(1+r/2)×((1+r)^N - 1)/r ] / (1+r)^N
         Lump_Sum = P_required - P_current (£200,000.00)
         FN = £750,000.00, r = 0.045000, C = £18,000.00/year

  Retire 1 year(s) earlier (2037, age 47): invest £33,492.18 today
  Retire 2 year(s) earlier (2036, age 46): invest £69,768.44 today
  Retire 3 year(s) earlier (2035, age 45): invest £109,021.67 today
  Retire 4 year(s) earlier (2034, age 44): invest £151,472.09 today
  Retire 5 year(s) earlier (2033, age 43): invest £197,369.82 today

  To use this for a specific target year T:
    N = T - currentYear
    compoundFactor = (1 + r)^N
    contribFV = C × (1 + r/2) × (compoundFactor - 1) / r
    P_required = (FN - contribFV) / compoundFactor
    Lump_Sum = P_required - P_current

FIRE PROJECTION:
  Current Portfolio Value: £200,000.00
  Target Value:            £750,000.00
  FIRE Year:               2038
  FIRE Age:                48

preferences.name: baseCurrency
preferences.title: Base Currency
preferences.description: Currency used to display total portfolio value. All holdings will be converted to this currency.

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
